title: Fix Get Location button: add stopPropagation and visible error toast
description: The Get Location button click fires but geolocation is silently denied in iframe/preview contexts. The current error feedback (a brief red border flash) is too subtle — users see no response at all. Need to add clear visible feedback.

plan.summary: Fix two issues with the Get Location button: (1) Add e.stopPropagation() to prevent the button click from also triggering the map's click-to-move handler. (2) Replace the subtle red border flash with a visible toast notification that tells the user why geolocation failed and suggests opening the app in a new tab. Also add a brief tooltip on the button itself explaining what it does. The toast should auto-dismiss after 5 seconds and match the dark nautical theme.

steps.description: Update sailing-weather.js: add e.stopPropagation() in the handleGetLocation click handler, replace the subtle .error class with a showToast() helper that displays a visible message like 'Location access denied — try opening this app in a new browser tab'. Also show the toast when geolocation is unavailable entirely. (req 1, 2)

Update sailing-weather.css: add styles for a .toast notification element — positioned near the bottom-right above the action buttons, with a slide-in animation, dark nautical theme, and auto-fade-out after 5 seconds. Remove the #location-btn.error style since we're replacing it with the toast. (req 2)
Update sailing-app template: add a #toast container element to the HTML for the notification messages. (req 2)
Screenshot and verify clicking the button shows a visible toast message. (req 1, 2)
